title: TSS competent cells and transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"du8rn","text":"This protocol allows the efficient transformation of plasmid into E. coli and related strains.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Also, over time, TSS can seem to be contaminated without being so, since the polyethylene glycol tends to precipitate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Grow o/n a 5 mL culture of the strain to be transformed. 5 mL 37 °C Overnight
Dilute 1:100 of the preinoculum in 50 mL prewarmed LB (OD 600 = 0,05). 50 mL 37 °C
Grow at 37ºC with agitation (170 rpm) until it reaches OD 600 = 0,5. 170 rpm, 37 °C,
Centrifuge the culture at 4000 g and 4ºC for 10 min. 4000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Recovery: add 250 µl of LB and incubate at 37ºC for 1 h. 37 °C 01:00:00
Plate 100 µl of the solution on LB agar plates supplemented with the indicated antibiotic and incubate o/n at 37ºC. 37 °C Overnight
Discard supernatant and wash with 1 mL of chilled TSS. 1 mL On ice
Incubate 10 min on ice. On ice 00:10:00
Discard supernatant and resuspend in 1 mL of TSS. 1 mL On ice
Incubate 30 min on ice. On ice 00:30:00
Thermal shock: incubate at 42ºC for 90 s. 42 °C 00:01:30
Centrifuge at 4000 g and 4ºC for 3 min. 4000 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes
Mix 5 μL of 5x KCM solution with DNA (around 1-5 μL) and H 2 O to a total of a 25 μL mixture. 25 µL
Mix the mixture with 25 μL of competent cells. 25 µL
Incubate 2 min on ice. On ice 00:02:00
creator: David Ruano Gallego
